steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The second clause restates the first: both describe the researchers determining plant proportions in the pronghorn diet. This is a restatement or clarification, not a new idea, sequence, or contrast.

steps.title: Match the signal to the correct answer
steps.content:
The logical relationship is restatement/clarification: the second clause rephrases the first in plainer terms ("relative quantities" = "ratio").
  • Choice A — "In addition,": signals a new, separate point being added. The second clause adds no new information — it only restates. ✗
  • Choice B — "Next,": signals a sequential step. The second clause is not a subsequent action; it explains the same action. ✗
  • Choice C — "By contrast,": signals opposition. The two clauses describe the same thing, not opposites. ✗
  • Choice D — "That is,": signals a restatement or clarification — "in other words." Fits the second clause redefining the first in simpler language. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
